channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump taps immigration hard-liner Kari Lake as head of Voice of America
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump said Wednesday that he's picking Kari Lake as director of Voice of America, installing a staunch loyalist who ran unsuccessfully for Arizona governor and a Senate seat to head the congressionally funded broadcaster that provides independent news reporti
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lombardo joins GOP governors 'fully committed' to Trump deportation threats
item.description: Gov. Joe Lombardo and 25 other Republican governors lined up in support of President-elect Donald Trump's immigration policies on Wednesday, calling for the deportation of "individuals who pose a threat.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Two lawmakers race to expand gun laws
item.description: Concerns about gun violence in St. Pete come as State Representative Dr. Joel Rudman and State Senator Randy Fine juggle gun laws in Tallahassee amid running for Congressional districts 1 and 6.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Secretary of State Blinken is returning to the Mideast in his latest diplomatic foray
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Antony Blinken is returning to the Middle East this week on his 12th visit since the Israel-Hamas war erupted last year but his first since the ouster of Syrian President Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jim Jordan cheers Wray resignation, but says he's not done probing his FBI tenure
item.description: House Judiciary Committee Chairman Jim Jordan is welcoming the news of FBI Director Christopher Wray's resignation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New data reveals America has tens of thousands of noncitizens from US adversary with deportation orders
item.description: Tens of thousands of Chinese nationals are among the more than 1.4 million noncitizens with final orders of removal but who are not in ICE custody, according to new figures.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hegseth meets with moderate Sen. Collins as he lobbies for key votes in the Senate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Pete Hegseth, President-elect Donald Trump’s nominee to lead the Defense Department, said he had a “wonderful conversation” with Maine Sen. Susan Collins on Wednesday as he pushed to win enough votes for confirmation.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New poll reveals what Americans think of Trump's transition decisions
item.description: Roughly half of Americans approve of how President-elect Trump is handling his transition to a second term in the White House, according to two new national polls.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House approves mammoth annual defense policy bill
item.description: The House passed the annual defense policy bill Wednesday, sending the mammoth measure to the Senate for consideration ahead of Congress’s end-of-month deadline.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Newsom sets special election dates for vacant 32nd District Assembly seat
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom announced a special election date for the vacant 32nd District Assembly.
item.dc:creator: Jose Franco, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans looking for new ways to force through China crackdowns left out of yearly defense bill
item.description: After a number of China crackdown provisions failed to make it into the yearly defense bill, Republicans are working on ways to get them signed into law before the end of the year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hakeem Jeffries says he's 'prepared to find common ground' with Trump next year
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries said addressing the high cost of living for Americans could be an area of bipartisan cooperation next year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FBI Director Wray to resign at end of Biden administration
item.description: FBI Director Christopher Wray on Wednesday announced he would resign from the bureau at the end of the Biden administration next month, with more than two years remaining on his term in office.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson and Ryan Lucas, MPR News

item.title: House approves $895B defense bill with military pay raise, ban on transgender care for minors
item.description: The House has passed a measure that authorizes a 1% increase in spending this fiscal year and a double-digit pay raise for about half of the enlisted service members in the military.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Jeffries: Democrats not telling members how to vote on NDAA
item.description: House Democratic leaders will not tell their members how to vote on the measure to reauthorize Pentagon spending that is hitting the floor Wednesday afternoon. House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sanders says this will likely be his last term
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.), one of the Senate’s most prominent progressives, says he likely won’t run again for the Senate after his fourth term expires in 2030.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP senators say they won’t fight Trump on Jan. 6 pardons
item.description: Republican senators who witnessed firsthand the Jan. 6, 2021, ransacking of congressional offices now say it’s up to President-elect Donald Trump whether to pardon those protesters convicted of federal crimes.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nancy Mace allegedly attacked on Capitol Hill; suspect arrested
item.description: Mace posted about the incident on X, saying, "One new brace for my wrist and some ice for my arm, and it’ll heal just fine.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Trump taps FTC Commissioner Andrew Ferguson to chair agency
item.description: President-elect Trump has tapped Andrew Ferguson, a commissioner at the Federal Trade Commission (FTC), as the next head of the independent agency.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Report on attempts to kill Trump urges Secret Service to limit protection of foreign leaders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A congressional task force investigating the attempts to kill Donald Trump during his presidential campaign is recommending changes to the Secret Service, including protecting fewer foreign leaders during the height of election season and considering moving the agency out of the Ho
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden to tout economic legacy in Tuesday speech
item.description: President Biden will deliver remarks Tuesday afternoon touting his economic agenda and making the case that it has been more effective than the kinds of tax cuts likely to be implemented by the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Mark Ruffalo presses Biden to act fast on 'forever chemicals' before Trump takes office
item.description: "The EPA has worked their butts off, against all odds, to get a drinking water standard on this particular chemical class," Ruffalo said.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, NewsNation

item.title: Pelosi says she backs generational change on committees, but there are exceptions
item.description: Rep. Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.) said this week that she supports the push for younger Democrats to replace veteran lawmakers at the top of committees — but there is at least one glaring exception to the rule.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: House GOP to choose chairs for influential committees: Who’s in the running
item.description: House Republican leaders will choose new chairs for a number of influential committees this week, people who will have the power to help deliver on President-elect Trump’s priorities in the GOP trifecta over the next two years.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Biden is rushing aid to Ukraine. Both sides are digging in. And everyone is bracing for Trump
item.description: The grinding war pitting Ukraine against its Russian invaders has escalated ahead of Donald Trump’s inauguration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard, Trump's pick for intel chief, faces questions on Capitol Hill amid Syria fallout
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump's pick for intelligence chief, Tulsi Gabbard, faced fresh scrutiny Monday on Capitol Hill about her proximity to Russian-ally Syria amid the sudden collapse of that country's hardline Assad rule.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada adds blockchain security to prevent repeat of 'fake electors' mischief
item.description: Nevada has integrated blockchain technology into its election certification process to add even more security to its procedures, the Nevada Secretary of State's Office announced on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida State Rep. Susan Valdes, representing parts of Hillsborough County, switches political parties
item.description: State Rep. Susan Valdes, who represents Florida's 64th District, including parts of Hillsborough County, switched political parties, the Florida House of Representatives announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Congressman Torres blasts crime in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The cold-blooded shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson in the heart of Manhattan shocked New Yorkers, including Congressman Ritchie Torres, leaving the lawmaker to pose the question: “What is happening in New York City?” Torres joined PIX on Politics to discuss city crim
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez seeks to lead Democrats on Oversight panel
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) on Friday formally launched a bid to lead Democrats on the Oversight and Accountability Committee in the next Congress — a powerful seat that will play a prominent role in the party's effort to push back against President Trump in his second term.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, Fox 5

item.title: Former national security officials urge caution on Gabbard
item.description: A group of nearly 100 former national security officials urged the Senate to “carefully scrutinize” former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), President-elect Trump’s nominee to serve as director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: A court upholds the U.S. ban on TikTok
item.description: The appeals court agreed that the viral video app must be sold off by its China-based parent company, rejecting TikTok's claim that the crackdown violates the free speech rights of millions of Americans.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Hegseth faces senators' concerns not only about his behavior but also his views on women in combat
item.description: Minnesotan Pete Hegseth has spent the week on Capitol Hill trying to reassure Republican senators that he is fit to lead President-elect Donald Trump’s Department of Defense in the wake of high-profile allegations about excessive drinking and sexual assault.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
